stories.title: Angels 2-1 A's — Neto Walk-Off HR After Being No-Hit Through 8 Innings (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
J.T. Ginn: 8 innings. No hits. 10 strikeouts. 1-0 lead.

Adam Frazier: infield single to break up the no-hitter.

Zach Neto: 2-run walk-off homer. Angels 2, Athletics 1.

Baseball is unbelievable.

#Angels #Athletics #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The Angels were no-hit through 8 innings and still won.

That's only happened 3 times since 1961.

Neto's walk-off last night joins one of baseball's rarest lists. 🔥

#Angels #MLB #BaseballHistory

facebook.long_form:
J.T. Ginn threw 8 no-hit innings last night. 105 pitches. 10 strikeouts. A 1-0 lead. He fanned the side in the seventh. He was in complete control.

Then the ninth inning happened.

Adam Frazier led off with an infield single — the first hit of the game, killing the no-hitter in an instant. Three pitches later, Zach Neto drove a 2-0 sinker 413 feet to center field. Two-run walk-off homer. Angels 2, Athletics 1.

Ginn's masterpiece evaporated in the span of one inning.

What makes this so remarkable: according to baseball historians, this is only the third time since 1961 that a team has been no-hit through at least 8 innings and still won — let alone on a walk-off homer. The previous instances were Don Lock of the Washington Senators in 1963 and Josh Harrison of the Pittsburgh Pirates in 2017 (extras). Ginn became just the sixth MLB pitcher since 1974 to allow no hits and no runs through 8 innings and still take the loss.

For the Angels, the win snapped a six-game losing streak. And it preserved a streak that most fans don't know about: Los Angeles hasn't been no-hit since September 11, 1999 — the longest active no-hit drought in the majors.

Zach Neto's walk-off blast. One of the most remarkable single-game moments of the 2026 season. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: J.T. Ginn was 8 innings into a no-hitter with a 1-0 lead. The Angels won 2-1 on a Zach Neto walk-off homer. Baseball doesn't get more dramatic than this. ⚾ #Angels #Athletics #MLB

article.headline: From Near No-Hitter to Walk-Off: Neto's Blast Stuns Ginn and the A's

stories.title: Dodgers 9-1 Phillies — Yamamoto 10 Ks, Ward First Career HR (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Ryan Ward spent 7 years in the Dodgers system.

Last night at Dodger Stadium, he finally hit his first career home run.

Yamamoto struck out 10. The Dodgers scored in 7 straight innings. Final: 9-1.

#Dodgers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Ryan Ward has spent seven years in the Los Angeles Dodgers organization. He's 28 years old. He's been called up, sent back down, called up again, and spent countless hours developing in the minor leagues while waiting for his shot.

Last night at Dodger Stadium, he got it.

Ward stepped up against Andrew Painter in the fourth inning and launched a 400-foot line drive to right field — an exit velocity of 108.4 mph — for the first home run of his major league career. The crowd roared. His teammates celebrated. Seven years of waiting, delivered in one swing.

It was the kind of moment that makes baseball worth watching.

The rest of the game wasn't bad either. Yoshinobu Yamamoto put together his best start of the season — 5.1 scoreless innings, 10 strikeouts, continuing a stretch that has him at a 2.48 ERA. The Dodgers scored in seven consecutive innings. Alex Freeland homered and drove in two. Max Muncy added his own homer. Bryson Stott hit a solo shot in the ninth to prevent a shutout, but the game was long over.

Final: Dodgers 9, Phillies 1. Los Angeles wins the series 2-1, bouncing back from Tanner Scott's blown save on Saturday.

For Ryan Ward, last night was finally the night. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Seven years in the minor leagues. Ryan Ward finally hit his first career home run last night at Dodger Stadium. Yamamoto struck out 10. Dodgers win 9-1. ⚾ #Dodgers #MLB

article.headline: Seven Years in the Making: Ryan Ward Hits First Career Homer in Dodgers' 9-1 Rout

stories.title: Cardinals 5-1 Cubs — Liberatore Bounces Back on Sunday Night Baseball (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Matthew Liberatore gave up 9+ runs in his last 3 starts combined.

Last night on Sunday Night Baseball: 5.1 shutout innings.

Cardinals 5, Cubs 1. St. Louis wins the series. Masyn Winn was the difference.

#STLCards #Cubs #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Matthew Liberatore was not having a good stretch. He'd allowed three or more runs in each of his previous three starts — not the kind of stretch you want heading into a Sunday Night Baseball appearance.

Last night on NBC, he was excellent.

Liberatore allowed just three hits and a walk in 5.1 scoreless innings, bouncing back in exactly the spot his team needed him. Masyn Winn drove in two with a single. JJ Wetherholt and Iván Herrera each collected two hits and scored twice. Alec Burleson added two more RBI. Hunter Dobbins — recalled from Triple-A Memphis that same day — came on for 3.2 innings and picked up his first professional save.

The Cardinals beat the Cubs 5-1 and won the series 2-1. St. Louis entered the series on a four-game losing streak. They leave it feeling good about their rotation and their offense.

Alex Bregman hit a solo homer for Chicago in the seventh — a reminder of how much the Cubs are counting on his comeback season. But Jordan Wicks (0-2) couldn't keep the game close.

Cardinals and Cubs: always must-watch, never dull. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Liberatore was 0-9 in his last 3 combined stat line. Last night on Sunday Night Baseball: 5.1 shutout innings. Cardinals 5, Cubs 1. Masyn Winn was the story. ⚾ #STLCards #Cubs #MLB

article.headline: Liberatore Silences the Doubters as Cardinals Top Cubs 5-1 on Sunday Night Baseball

stories.title: Tigers Rotation in Crisis — Mize Joins Skubal and Verlander on IL (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Detroit Tigers currently have three starting pitchers on the IL.

Tarik Skubal (elbow surgery, out through July).
Justin Verlander (hip, eligible to return).
Casey Mize (adductor, just placed on IL).

That's their No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 starters. Rough.

#Tigers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Just when you thought Detroit's pitching situation couldn't get more complicated, it got more complicated.

The Tigers placed right-hander Casey Mize on the 15-day injured list on Friday (retroactive to May 28) with right adductor inflammation. Mize joins Tarik Skubal and Justin Verlander — giving Detroit three starting pitchers on the injured list at the same time.

Skubal, who underwent surgery to remove a loose body from his left elbow, is conservatively expected to be out through July. The procedure used a device called a NanoNeedle — a smaller instrument than traditional arthroscopy — and Skubal is the first MLB player to have the surgery with it. The hope is for a faster recovery, but the timeline remains cautious.

Verlander was eligible to come off the 60-day IL at the end of May. He has a scheduled rehab assignment with the Triple-A Toledo Mud Hens. Detroit is hoping to get the future Hall of Famer back on the major league mound in June, but the rotation around him is threadbare.

And now Mize is out, too.

The Tigers had legitimate playoff aspirations entering 2026. Whether those aspirations survive losing three starters simultaneously is the defining question of their season. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Skubal. Verlander. Now Mize. The Detroit Tigers have three starting pitchers on the injured list at the same time. Their rotation is in crisis. ⚾ #Tigers #MLB

article.headline: Three Starters, One IL: Detroit's Rotation Crisis Deepens With Mize Injury

stories.title: Schwarber's Historic HR Pace — Still MLB's Sole Leader With Murakami Out (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber hit 20 home runs in the Phillies' first 49 games.

On pace for 66 on the season.

The only players who have been better through 49 games? Barry Bonds in 2001. Mark McGwire in 1998.

#Phillies #MLB #HomeRunRace

facebook.long_form:
Let's talk about what Kyle Schwarber is doing — because the numbers are historically rare.

Schwarber hit his 20th home run of the 2026 season in the Phillies' 49th game. That makes him just the 18th player in MLB history to reach 20 home runs that quickly. The last player to do it was Josh Hamilton in 2012.

And here's where the comparison gets truly staggering: the only players in the modern era who have been BETTER than Schwarber's current pace through 49 games are Barry Bonds in 2001 (25 HRs) and Mark McGwire in 1998 (25 HRs). Those are the two seasons that produced the single-season home run records of 73 and 70.

Schwarber's current pace projects to 66 home runs for the 2026 season.

With Munetaka Murakami (who had tied Schwarber at the top of the HR leaderboard before going down with a hamstring injury) expected out until mid-June, Schwarber stands alone as the MLB home run leader. The nearest challengers are nowhere close.

Can he sustain it? That's the only question. Big power starts don't always hold. But right now, Kyle Schwarber is doing something that only two players in baseball history have done better — and those two players are in the record books. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: 20 HRs in 49 games. On pace for 66. Kyle Schwarber is doing something only Bonds and McGwire have done better. The HR race is his right now. ⚾ #Phillies #MLB #HomeRunRace

article.headline: Bonds, McGwire, Schwarber: The Historic Company He's Keeping Right Now
